rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARCIA BLANCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/03/2020. A supplement signed and verified by the petitioner addressing the following issues must be filed: 1) A detailed schedule of receipts must be filed as the statutory compensation is calculated based on an amount other than the inventory and appraisal value. CRC 7.550(b)(6)(information required even if an account has been waived). 2) Exhibits A-C show the cash on hand in the amount of $618,521.23, but the bank statement shows the cash balance of $674,829.37 as of 02/25/2020. 3) Has the petitioner been allowing Wells Fargo Bank to hold the estate funds far exceeding the FDIC limits since February 2020?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALFRED LORD JR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Persons Entitled To Distribution Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/17/20. No supporting documentation has been filed either by the administrator or by the parties purporting to be heirs to support any heirship. The Court cannot find and order that Gaither Lord was a half-brother of the decedent without any evidence. Any documentary evidence must be filed in time to provide a courtesy copy to the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/6/20.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WAYNE R EDMUNDS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/3/20. If the statutory fee is based on any amount other than the I&A, detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sales must be included. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] Attorney costs advanced must be itemized; "Court Filing Fees" in a lump sum is not sufficient. The Probate Referee's commission was $1,250.00 according to the I&A, (Item 4 of the I&A should NOT be completed when a Probate Referee WAS appointed.) A supplement to the petition, signed and verified by the administrator, must be filed to address these issues. A courtesy copy of the supplement must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/23/20.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LEILA CHIRAYATH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/4/20. No proposed order. [LRSF 14.6 A.] Proposed orders must be submitted as hard copies, not E-filed. No endorsed filed copy of the proof of service was provided; courtesy copies are endorsed filed copies. Examiners will not review papers that have not been filed. [LRSF 14.6 D.] A supplement to the petition must be filed, signed and verified by the petitioner, submitting (1) an Attachment 7 and (2) supporting documentation showing how title is held to the assets at issue (vehicles and real property). A courtesy copy of the supplement and a proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/26/20.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EVELYN NELSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/10/20. No courtesy copy of the amended petition has been provided. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.93 G.] An amending pleading requires the same notice as the pleading it amends, including publication. [CRC 7.53(a).] Published notice must clearly indicate that the hearing is for an AMENDED petition (which was not filed until 9/22/20, so publication starting on 9/21/20 was also defective for the amended petition). In a verified supplement, prepared on pleading paper, the petitioner must clarify item 3d(2)(a) of the petition, which shows that annual gross income from real property is $800,000, and must provide the missing information from items 5a(3) or (4), 5a(7) or (8), and 5b of the petition. A courtesy (endorsed filed, hard) copy of the correct proof of publication of notice of the amended petition and of the supplement to the petition must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 10/30/20.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CRISTIAN A. SOLIS-ESCUDERO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/10/20. The incorrect (outdated) form of Notice of Petition to Administer Estate was served and published. The Waiver of Bond is a mandatory Judicial Council form. The petitioner (the person named in item 2 of the petition) is missing from item 8 of the petition. By no later than 10/30/20, courtesy copies of the following must be received by the assigned Examiner: 1. Proof of service and of publication of the correct Notice of Petition to Administer (showing the 11/10/20 hearing date). 2. A supplement to the petition, signed and verified by the proposed administrator, completing item 8 of the petition. 3. Correct waivers of bond.
